[2020-06-10 18:16] .bky_1556 (bky): I've had only one game ever where I was sure I lost to setup RNG rather than my own flawed play.
[2020-06-10 18:19] siepu: yeah the idea is that when you both are supergood a lot of sets are flawed
[2020-06-10 18:19] siepu: but this 'a lot' is like.. 30% and it feels it's evenly divided between p1 and p2
[2020-06-10 18:20] siepu: if prismata used bridge sistem in the tournaments I think it's fair enough
[2020-06-10 18:22] amalloy: tbh every game with random setup should use duplicate scoring
[2020-06-10 18:23] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): what's the bridge system and duplicate scoring?
[2020-06-10 18:23] .bky_1556 (bky): Each team has two players.
[2020-06-10 18:23] .bky_1556 (bky): The players on the team each play one side of the matchup.
[2020-06-10 18:24] .bky_1556 (bky): Scoring is basically based on which team won harder on the advantaged side.
[2020-06-10 18:24] amalloy: that's one way to play duplicate, which would be good for tournaments
[2020-06-10 18:24] siepu: bridge is every player plays the same sets
[2020-06-10 18:24] .bky_1556 (bky): The actual bridge system is more complex than that, though.
[2020-06-10 18:24] siepu: so it doesnt matter that some are player favoured
[2020-06-10 18:25] siepu: what matters is how better/worse than expected you did
[2020-06-10 18:25] amalloy: @bky it's really not. you can just play BAM for a team game, or matchpoints for something close to ladder play
[2020-06-10 18:25] amalloy: you don't need the complicated stuff like IMPs, for a win/lose game like prismata
[2020-06-10 18:26] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): thanks. interesting. replaying sets would be nice
[2020-06-10 18:26] siepu: i dont like replaying in prismata at all
[2020-06-10 18:26] .bky_1556 (bky): The problem with replaying sets is strategy copying.
[2020-06-10 18:26] .bky_1556 (bky): If the apparently disadvantaged player finds a strong but counterintuitive line in game 1, they can expect to face it in game 2.
[2020-06-10 18:27] .bky_1556 (bky): Even if their opponent is very bad at finding such strategies normally.
